meta.sctld_alert: Stony Coral Tissue Loss Disease (SCTLD) detected on Bonaire reefs. Disinfect gear before first dive and after last dive of trip. Do NOT use gloves or pointer sticks.

conditions_june_july.current: Generally mild to none; south sites can have moderate current
conditions_june_july.wind: Trade winds from ENE, typically 15-20 knots in summer
conditions_june_july.hurricane_risk: Bonaire is south of hurricane belt - minimal risk

sources: Published dive guides: Lonely Planet Diving & Snorkeling Bonaire (Lotts/Watrous), Reef Smart Guides Bonaire
Community dive logs and diver reports
notes: Coordinates: ~34 sites have GPS from PADI/published sources; remaining sites have approximate coordinates based on known location relative to landmarks. Mark with conf=estimated.
Klein Bonaire sites: boat-only access (~800m offshore from Kralendijk). No shore diving due to fragile/shallow reef.
Shore dive sites marked with yellow painted rocks along the road.
Mooring buoys at each site; boats limited to 2 hours per buoy.
Night diving: excellent at most shore sites. Tanks refill stations open 24 hours.
Visibility and depth data verified against multiple published sources.

regions.west_coast_north: Sites north of Kralendijk up to Washington Slagbaai (currently closed for diving)
regions.west_coast_south: Sites south of Kralendijk to Punt Vierkant / Pink Beach / Red Slave
regions.klein_bonaire: All boat-only sites around Klein Bonaire islet
regions.east_coast: Limited dive sites; exposed to trade wind chop

dive_sites.name: 1000 Steps (Piedra Haltu)
dive_sites.notes: Named for 67 rock steps leading to entry (feels like 1000 on exit). One of the most famous shore dives in the world. North end of the double reef area. Star coral formations are spectacular.
dive_sites.route: Enter via steps, swim west/northwest to reef crest at 5-7m, drop down wall to 20-30m. Return along wall then shallower on reef top.
dive_sites.access: Parking area off Kaya Karpata (north road). Steps cut into cliff.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Bring booties for the steps. Easy self-guided. Excellent macro in crevices.

dive_sites.name: Karpata
dive_sites.notes: Regarded as one of the best wide-angle underwater photography sites in the world. Famous for massive elkhorn coral formations and old anchors covered in coral growth. Very scenic wall. Old anchors from pre-park era visible in coral.
dive_sites.route: Entry over boulders/rocky shore. Swim west to wall drop. Visibility and wide-angle views are best feature.
dive_sites.access: Small parking area off north coastal road near the STINAPA station.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Surge can be strong on windy days. Best in calm conditions. Fantastic for photography.

dive_sites.name: Ol' Blue (Tolo)
dive_sites.notes: Known as Tolo or Ol' Blue. Nice wall dive. Good for drift diving when there is current. Name comes from the blue-toned visibility.
dive_sites.route: Shore entry, swim west, reef starts at ~10m. Can drift south along wall.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good intermediate wall dive. Check current direction before entering.

dive_sites.name: Andrea I
dive_sites.notes: Part of the Andrea series of sites. Moderate depth reef with nice coral coverage.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Reliable beginner/intermediate shore dive.

dive_sites.name: Andrea II
dive_sites.notes: Companion site to Andrea I. Similar profile. Look for macro life on sandy bottom.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good macro dive. Often less visited than famous sites.

dive_sites.name: Aquarius
dive_sites.notes: Shallow to moderate reef. Great for beginners and macro enthusiasts. Sandy areas host flounder and other benthic species.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good for newer divers or post-certification practice.

dive_sites.name: Red Beryl
dive_sites.notes: Named for the distinctive red coral species found here. Drop-off wall with sandy shelf.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Look for the red beryl (ruby coral) formations.

dive_sites.name: La Dania's Leap
dive_sites.notes: Named for a famous cliff-jump entry. Entry from a high rocky ledge - must leap/jump in. Dramatic wall. Archaeological feature nearby.
dive_sites.route: Jump entry from ledge, descend to wall. Can drift north or south.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Entry is a jump from elevated rocks - not recommended if any swell. Check conditions carefully. Exciting site for advanced divers.

dive_sites.name: Ol' Blue (Klein Bonaire side)
dive_sites.notes: Note: may be the same as site #3 listed under different name in some guides. Verify with local dive shop.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Confirm with dive operator.

dive_sites.name: Country Garden
dive_sites.notes: Named for diverse coral garden in shallows. Good for macro and wide-angle.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Often overlooked; lovely coral garden in 5-15m.

dive_sites.name: Forest
dive_sites.notes: Dense coral formations resembling an underwater forest. Good sponge life.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Dense coral coverage - watch buoyancy.

dive_sites.name: Rappel
dive_sites.notes: Named for the abseil rope to help access entry point (steep cliff). Actually not commonly used now.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Rocky entry requiring care.

dive_sites.name: Oil Slick Leap
dive_sites.notes: Entry by jumping off a concrete platform (the 'leap'). Former oil tanker mooring. Excellent wall with soft corals and sea fans. Named for former petroleum operations at site.
dive_sites.route: Jump from concrete platform, descend wall going south. Can return via shallower reef.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Classic Bonaire jump entry. Good for photos. Do not jump if waves are breaking on platform. Night dive is excellent for tarpon hunting.
dive_sites.access: Small parking area, concrete platform built over the sea.

dive_sites.name: Barcadera
dive_sites.notes: Near Barcadera harbor (water intake area). Good for macro. Some boat traffic - stay clear of the harbor area.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Macro diving - look for frogfish and seahorse.

dive_sites.name: Cliff
dive_sites.notes: Drop-off wall at edge of the cliff. Nice variety of life. Frogfish commonly spotted.
dive_sites.route: Entry over rocky shore. Swim west to wall edge. Dive south or north along wall.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good wall dive close to Kralendijk. Look for frogfish in crevices.

dive_sites.name: 18th Palm
dive_sites.notes: Named for the 18th palm tree on the beachfront. Gentle sloping reef. Great for snorkelers and new divers.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Ideal beginner/snorkeling site. Sandy beach entry.

dive_sites.name: Capt. Don's Reef
dive_sites.notes: House reef of Captain Don's Habitat resort. Named for Captain Don Stewart, father of Bonaire's marine park. Gentle sloping reef.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good reef for all levels. Easily accessible from the Habitat resort.

dive_sites.name: Reef Scientifico
dive_sites.notes: Near the scientific station on the waterfront. Nice shallow reef with good coral coverage.

dive_sites.name: Front Porch
dive_sites.notes: Small wreck site close to shore. Named for its proximity to a house's front porch. Short swim to wreck.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good for less experienced wreck divers. Accessible.

dive_sites.name: Town Pier (Kralendijk Town Pier)
dive_sites.notes: NIGHT DIVE ONLY or by special permission. Considered one of the world's best night dives. Located at the main cruise ship pier in Kralendijk. Pier pilings encrusted with life. Must get permission from port authority or through local dive shop. Often organized night dives available.
dive_sites.route: Entry from the dock, explore pilings at 3-10m. Very shallow but extraordinarily dense marine life.
dive_sites.trip_notes: DO NOT do this without permission - active commercial dock. Night dive essential - tarpon and frogfish are spectacular under the lights. Most operators organize group night dives here. MUST-DO on any Bonaire trip.
dive_sites.access: Requires permission from harbormaster. Most dive operators can arrange access.
dive_sites.special: WORLD-CLASS NIGHT DIVE - one of top 10 in Caribbean

dive_sites.name: Calabas (Buddy Dive House Reef)
dive_sites.notes: House reef of Buddy Dive Resort. Sloping reef to wall. Convenient access.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Easy shore dive right from Buddy Dive - great for morning/evening dives.

dive_sites.name: Cha Cha Cha
dive_sites.notes: Beautiful shallow reef slopes down to sandy areas with great variety of reef fish. Deeper sections accessible but most action 5-25m.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good for photography and fish ID. Wide variety in relatively small area.

dive_sites.name: Something Special
dive_sites.notes: Known for exceptional macro life. Frogfish, scorpionfish, and crustaceans abound. Lives up to its name.
dive_sites.trip_notes: MACRO SPECIALIST SITE. Must-do for photographers. Bring a good eye and patience.

dive_sites.name: Windsock Steep
dive_sites.notes: Steep wall near the airport windsock. Good wall structure with gorgonians and sponges.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Nice wall close to airport - easy access.

dive_sites.name: The Beach (Wannadive/Grand Windsock House Reef)
dive_sites.notes: House reef of Wannadive / Grand Windsock resort near airport. Very accessible sandy beach entry.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good for beginners and snorkeling. Easy access.

dive_sites.name: Yellow Sub
dive_sites.notes: Named for a yellow submarine prop. Gentle reef. Good for beginners.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Easy dive near airport.

dive_sites.name: Flamingo Diving House Reef (Delfins Beach Resort)
dive_sites.notes: House reef of the old Flamingo Beach Resort (now Delfins Beach Resort). Reef and wall.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good house reef dive.

dive_sites.name: Bachelor's Beach (Beyond The Corals House Reef)
dive_sites.notes: House reef of Beyond The Corals resort. Gently sloping reef. Good for beginners. Tarpon often patrol the shallows at night.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Calm beach entry. Great night dive for tarpon.

dive_sites.name: Invisibles
dive_sites.notes: Named for the way the reef drops away invisibly. Excellent visibility. Great for garden eels on sandy flats.
dive_sites.route: Shore entry, swim to reef at ~8m then drop to deeper sections. Garden eels visible on sandy areas.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Excellent all-round dive. Garden eels on the sandy slope at ~20m are a highlight.

dive_sites.name: Tori's Reef
dive_sites.notes: Shallow, accessible reef. Channel feature draws pelagics. Easy entry. Named after a diver.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good family/beginner dive. Watch for eagle rays cruising through.

dive_sites.name: Margate Bay
dive_sites.notes: Beautiful bay with wall dive. Can have moderate current suitable for drift diving. Named after the bay.
dive_sites.route: Shore entry. Swim to wall and drift south along it if current is running.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Check current direction. Good drift when conditions right. Eagle rays common.

dive_sites.name: Salt Pier
dive_sites.notes: Under the active salt-loading pier. Massive concrete pilings encrusted with enormous orange and yellow encrusting sponges, cup corals, gorgonians. One of the most distinctive dives on Bonaire. Active industrial pier - CANNOT DIVE WHEN SALT SHIPS ARE LOADING. Check with dive shop on schedule.
dive_sites.route: Entry from shore near pier. Swim under the pier structure. All exploration under/around pilings 5-18m.
dive_sites.trip_notes: CHECK SALT SHIP SCHEDULE before going. Cannot dive when actively loading. Typically 2-3 times per week. This is a SPECTACULAR dive - pilings covered top to bottom in colorful marine life. One of the best in Bonaire. Day or night is excellent.
dive_sites.special: TOP SITE - iconic pier pilings covered in sponges and coral

dive_sites.name: Salt City
dive_sites.notes: Adjacent to Salt Pier. Reef diving in the salt production area. Good alternative when Salt Pier is closed.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good backup for Salt Pier days when ships are loading.

dive_sites.name: Punt Vierkant
dive_sites.notes: Dutch for 'square point.' Northern tip of the famous double reef system. Good wall dive. Transition point between double reef area and more open ocean.
dive_sites.route: Enter from shore, reach wall and dive south along it. Shallower double reef structure visible.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Gateway to the double reef system. Good for intermediate and advanced divers.

dive_sites.name: The Lake
dive_sites.notes: Named for the calm, lake-like conditions found between the two reef ridges of the double reef. Beautiful gorgonians in shallows. Circling schools of reef fish are spectacular. Between the outer and inner reef of the double reef system.
dive_sites.route: Enter from shore at the parking area. Swim to inner reef, then across sandy channel to outer reef, return along inner reef.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Excellent for all levels. The gorgonians in 8-12m are stunning. Schools of fish circle in the 'lake' area between reefs.

dive_sites.name: Angel City
dive_sites.notes: Named for the enormous schools of French angelfish found here. Sits between the double reef ridges. The Hilma Hooker wreck lies just to the south. Can combine with Hilma Hooker dive. One of Bonaire's most popular sites.
dive_sites.route: Enter from shore, swim to double reef structure. Hilma Hooker is ~100m south along the sand flat.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Combine with Hilma Hooker. Watch the French angelfish schools - spectacular. Sandy flats between reefs harbor garden eels.

dive_sites.name: Hilma Hooker (Wreck)
dive_sites.notes: 71.78m (235ft) Dutch cargo ship confiscated with 25,000 lbs of marijuana, sank accidentally September 12, 1984. Lies on starboard side in ~18m min / 30m keel / 40m max. Considered one of the best wreck dives in the Caribbean. Little penetration required - open decks. The hull is covered in coral and gorgonians. Bow is visible at ~18m, stern at ~30m.
dive_sites.route: Shore entry at Angel City parking. Swim southwest on sandy bottom (~100m) until wreck appears. Explore bow to stern at various depths. Return to shallower reef for a deco stop.
dive_sites.access: Park at Angel City. Sandy bottom swim.
dive_sites.trip_notes: MUST-DO wreck dive. Best at slack water. Upper superstructure at 18-20m accessible to OW divers. Full length swim at ~30m for advanced. Night dive is excellent. Combines well with Angel City.
dive_sites.special: One of top wreck dives in Caribbean

dive_sites.name: Alice in Wonderland
dive_sites.notes: Famous for enormous, surreal-looking elephant ear sponges and giant gorgonian sea fans that create an Alice-in-Wonderland-like landscape. Southern end of the double reef. One of Bonaire's most visually impressive sites.
dive_sites.route: Shore entry, swim to reef. Outer reef has the most dramatic giant sponges.
dive_sites.trip_notes: MUST-DO for sponge/gorgonian photography. Best in 15-30m. The giant elephant ear sponges are 1-2m across. Signature Bonaire site.
dive_sites.special: Famous for giant elephant ear sponges

dive_sites.name: Vista Blue
dive_sites.notes: Named for the excellent blue water visibility. Nice sloping reef. Cleaner shrimp stations make for good macro shots.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good all-round dive. Look for cleaning stations.

dive_sites.name: Sweet Dreams
dive_sites.notes: Gentle sloping reef. Good schooling fish. Named for the pleasant, easy dive experience.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Reliable, pleasant dive. Good for marine life variety.

dive_sites.name: Pink Beach
dive_sites.notes: Pink beach (actually pink-hued sand from coral fragments) with excellent diving. Shallow reef progresses to deeper wall. Beautiful setting. Also good for snorkeling.
dive_sites.route: Sandy beach entry. Reef starts at 5m. Wall drops off to 25m+.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Beautiful setting. Great for snorkeling too. Turtles very common. One of the most scenic beaches on Bonaire.
dive_sites.access: Gravel road south of Kralendijk, past salt works.

dive_sites.name: White Slave
dive_sites.notes: Near the historic White Slave huts (slave quarters obelisks - UNESCO heritage site). Sloping reef to sandy bottom with garden eels. Archaeological significance.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Historical interest above and below water. Good for seeing garden eels.
dive_sites.access: Located near historic slave huts (obelisks). Coastal road south.

dive_sites.name: Red Slave
dive_sites.notes: Near the Red Slave obelisks (historic slave quarters). Exposed south end of island. Can have significant current. Deep reef - requires advanced skills in strong current conditions. Access road is rough. Strong surge possible.
dive_sites.route: Rocky shore entry. Significant current possible - be experienced.
dive_sites.trip_notes: ADVANCED SITE when current is running. Check conditions. Exciting diving but requires experience. Often dived from boat for better control. Rare seahorse sightings here.

dive_sites.name: Lighthouse (Willemstoren)
dive_sites.notes: Southernmost tip of Bonaire near Willemstoren lighthouse. Very exposed with potential strong current. Remote. Pristine reef because it's rarely visited. Pelagics possible.
dive_sites.trip_notes: REMOTE AND ADVANCED. Best by boat. Must-do for advanced divers if conditions permit. Rarely touched reef.

dive_sites.name: Larry's Lair
dive_sites.notes: Named after a local diver. Sandy bottom with garden eels and reef sections. Very pleasant, quiet site.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good for garden eel photography. Quiet site.

dive_sites.name: Jeannie's Glory
dive_sites.notes: Named after a local diver. Varied reef with good fish life. Similar profile to nearby sites.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Pleasant dive. Good schooling fish.

dive_sites.name: Carl's Hill
dive_sites.notes: Named after Carl. Wall section with good sponge and gorgonian life.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good wall structure.

dive_sites.name: Atlantis
dive_sites.notes: Named playfully for the mythical underwater city. Good reef with active fish life. Eagle rays patrol regularly.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Watch for eagle rays on the sandy bottom. Reliable site.

dive_sites.name: Sweet Dreams (alternate name: Sweet Dreams South)
dive_sites.notes: May be same or adjacent to Sweet Dreams (#40). Confirm with local operator.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Verify with local operator.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - No Name Beach
dive_sites.notes: The most popular beach/snorkel landing on Klein Bonaire. Good snorkeling and diving from the beach. Also the only spot on Klein Bonaire where you can go ashore.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Boat taxi from Bonaire town (~10 min). Excellent for snorkeling. Bring water - no facilities on Klein Bonaire.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Hands Off
dive_sites.notes: Name means 'hands off' - keep off the fragile reef. Vertical wall and slope. One of several wall dives around Klein Bonaire.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Pristine reef. Excellent wall dive. Pure Klein Bonaire experience.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Just a Nice Dive
dive_sites.notes: Named quite literally. A relaxing, beautiful reef dive. One of the most loved on Klein Bonaire for its simplicity.
dive_sites.trip_notes: As the name says - a really nice dive. Perfect for those wanting an uncomplicated but beautiful experience.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Knife
dive_sites.notes: Sharp wall drop-off giving site its name. Good vertical wall with excellent coral coverage.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Nice wall dive. Good for wide-angle photography.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Keepsake
dive_sites.notes: A 'keepsake' memory dive - beautiful reef. Known for hawksbill turtle sightings.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good hawksbill turtle site.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - South Bay
dive_sites.notes: Protected bay on south side of Klein Bonaire. Gentle slopes and sandy areas.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good for all levels.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Ebo's Reef
dive_sites.notes: Named after a local diver Ebo. Beautiful coral reef on the eastern side of Klein Bonaire.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good coral coverage.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Sharon's Serenity
dive_sites.notes: Named for serene conditions. Shallow, peaceful reef. Good for beginners.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Calm conditions, good for beginner divers.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Mi Dushi
dive_sites.notes: 'Mi Dushi' means 'my sweetheart' in Papiamentu. Beautiful wall dive. Considered one of Klein Bonaire's best.
dive_sites.trip_notes: One of the best Klein Bonaire sites. Wall and reef.
dive_sites.special: One of the best Klein Bonaire sites

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Twixt
dive_sites.notes: Between other Klein Bonaire sites. Good coral garden.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Reliable reef dive.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Forest (Klein)
dive_sites.notes: Dense coral formations. One of Klein Bonaire's sites bearing the same name as a mainland site.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Distinctive coral garden.

dive_sites.name: Klein Bonaire - Leonora's Reef
dive_sites.notes: Named after a local diver. Beautiful shallow reef.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Pleasant reef dive.

dive_sites.name: Carib Inn House Reef
dive_sites.notes: House reef of Bruce Bowker's Carib Inn. Classic Bonaire shore dive.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Convenient house reef.

dive_sites.name: Sampler
dive_sites.notes: A 'sampler' of Bonaire's reef life. Good introduction dive. Nice starter for first dives on island.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Good orientation dive.

dive_sites.name: Buddy's Reef (Buddy Dive)
dive_sites.notes: House reef for Buddy Dive Resort. Convenient, well-maintained.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Easy access from Buddy Dive.

dive_sites.name: Windsock Steep / Airport (Carib Inn Area)
dive_sites.notes: Near the airport windsock indicator. Steep wall dive. Good gorgonians and sponges.
dive_sites.trip_notes: Wall dive near airport. Check it before flying home.

dive_sites.name: Boka Slagbaai (Washington Slagbaai)
dive_sites.notes: CURRENTLY CLOSED FOR DIVING per STINAPA notice. Washington Slagbaai National Park dive sites are not accessible as of 2025-2026. Check with STINAPA for updates before trip.
dive_sites.trip_notes: ⚠️ CLOSED - DO NOT PLAN THIS DIVE until confirmed open. Check stinapabonaire.org for updates.

best_night_dives: Town Pier (MUST DO - arrange in advance)

shore_dive_logistics.truck_rental: Rent a pickup truck - essential for self-guided shore diving. Load tanks in truck bed.
shore_dive_logistics.tank_fills: Available 24 hours at most dive operators (Buddy Dive, Divi Flamingo, etc.)
shore_dive_logistics.entry_fee_2026: Nature tag required - approximately $40 USD per person for trip (single annual tag covers all dives)
shore_dive_logistics.boat_dives: Arrange through local operators. Klein Bonaire requires boat. Most shops offer 2-tank boat dive packages.

conditions_june_july_specific.wetsuit: 3mm shorty or full suit for multiple dives per day
conditions_june_july_specific.rain: Occasional short afternoon showers, not disruptive
conditions_june_july_specific.current: Generally minimal on west coast; south sites (Red Slave, Lighthouse) can have moderate-strong current with southern swell
conditions_june_july_specific.surge: Minimal on most sites; can be present at La Dania's Leap and Oil Slick Leap on windy days
